steps.title: Set the equations equal and simplify
steps.content:
From the second equation, $y = 8x - 9$. Substitute into $y = x^2 + 4x - 5$:
$$x^2 + 4x - 5 = 8x - 9$$
Collecting all terms on one side:
$$x^2 - 4x + 4 = 0$$

steps.title: Apply the discriminant test
steps.content: For $x^2 - 4x + 4 = 0$, we have $a = 1$, $b = -4$, $c = 4$. The discriminant is: $$\Delta = b^2 - 4ac = (-4)^2 - 4(1)(4) = 16 - 16 = 0$$ Since $\Delta = 0$, there is exactly one real solution. C is correct.
